# Synthetic Data

*Training data generated by models rather than scraped from the world — increasingly how labs manufacture the high-quality, targeted data that scraping can't supply.*

## What it is
Model-generated examples used to train or post-train other models: self-instruct-style instruction sets, distilled reasoning traces, textbook-quality synthetic corpora, and agentic-task trajectories. It spans the pipeline — synthetic pre-training data ([[pretraining-data]]) and, more commonly, synthetic post-training data ([[sft]], [[reward-modeling]]). It's the connective tissue between [[distillation]] (a strong model's outputs become a weaker model's training set) and the broader move away from purely human-authored data.

## Why it matters
Synthetic data is how labs escape the data wall: when high-quality human text runs short, you generate more — targeted at exactly the capabilities you want. For the exec the implication is that *data is becoming a compute problem*, not a scraping problem, which favors whoever already has the strongest model (they can mint the best training data for the next one — a flywheel). The risk is model collapse and quality drift if synthetic data isn't grounded in real signal.

## What the evidence shows
**Foundations.** Self-Instruct showed a model can bootstrap its own instruction-tuning data (arXiv:2212.10560); "Textbooks Are All You Need" (phi) showed synthetic textbook-quality data can punch far above its weight class (arXiv:2306.11644); Distilling Step-by-Step turned a teacher's rationales into training signal for much smaller students (arXiv:2305.02301).

**Recent developments.** The agentic frontier uses synthetic data plus scalable RL to bridge to proprietary-grade agents → census work like WebSailor-V2 (arXiv:2509.13305). On the practitioner side, the discipline of grounding synthetic data in real traces (so it doesn't drift from reality) is argued in [[2026-03-26-blog-husain-revenge-data-scientist]].

## Relationships
- feeds [[pretraining-data]], [[sft]], [[reward-modeling]]; stage input to [[frontier-model-training]]
- mechanism behind [[distillation]]
- practitioner view from [[hamel-husain]]

## Key papers (full-text ingested)
- [[2022-12-20-arxiv-2212-10560-self-instruct|Self-Instruct]] — 52K instructions from a 175-task seed; +33pt on Super-NaturalInstructions
- [[2023-06-20-arxiv-2306-11644-textbooks-are-all-you-need-phi-1|Textbooks Are All You Need (phi-1)]] — 1.3B on ~7B synthetic tokens hits 50.6% HumanEval, beating GPT-3.5
- [[2023-05-03-arxiv-2305-02301-distilling-step-by-step|Distilling Step-by-Step]] — 770B teacher rationales let a 770M student beat 540B PaLM on ANLI with 80% of the data
- [[2025-09-16-arxiv-2509-13305-websailor-v2|WebSailor-V2]] — synthetic data + scalable RL on a 30B-A3B MoE beats 671B DeepSeek-V3.1 on BrowseComp
